metric_name: Validation rework rate (proxy: invalid applications returned)
notes: Baseline derived from PS2 invalid/withdrawn application proportions

source_dataset: GOV.UK Table P152 quality of major decisions
source_row_ref: P152 appeals allowed as pct of decisions
metric_name: Appeal overturn rate for major housing refusals
baseline_value: Approx 1.5-2.5 percent of total decisions overturned at appeal
notes: Quality indicator measuring LPA decision quality via appeal outcomes

source_dataset: NPPF revision history and PPG update log
source_row_ref: NPPF publication dates vs local plan adoption dates
metric_name: Policy conflict incidents (proxy: plans older than 5 years vs current NPPF)
baseline_value: Over 50 percent of local plans are more than 5 years old
notes: Synchronisation gap between central policy updates and local plan review cycles

source_dataset: GOV.UK Table P151 speed of major decisions
metric_name: Median determination time for EIA/habitats-affected major schemes
baseline_value: Approx 40-60 percent decided within statutory period for major applications
notes: Proxy for environmental assessment delay impact on determination speed

metric_name: NSIP examination duration and s78 appeal determination time
baseline_value: Median NSIP examination 15-18 months; s78 appeal 6-12 months
notes: Infrastructure pathway baseline from Planning Inspectorate casework data

source_dataset: Pilot LPA validation and design policy review
source_row_ref: Local plan design policy compliance data (pilot LPAs)
baseline_value: Estimated 50-60 percent of applications comply with local design policies on first submission
notes: Baseline estimated from pilot LPA officer feedback and refusal reason analysis

source_dataset: GOV.UK Table P153 speed of non-major decisions
metric_name: Pre-commencement condition discharge determination time (proxy: householder application speed)
baseline_value: 88 percent of non-major applications decided in time nationally
notes: Condition discharge has no statutory period; proxy via non-major speed table

source_row_ref: MHCLG housing delivery statistics and s106 monitoring
metric_name: Median time from planning resolution to legal completion
baseline_value: Median s106 completion delay estimated at 9-18 months based on monitoring data
notes: s106 delays documented in MHCLG delivery evidence and practitioner surveys

source_dataset: GOV.UK DMPO and PINS ministerial measures
source_row_ref: PINS ministerial measures valid on first submission rate
metric_name: Statutory consultee response rate within 21-day period
baseline_value: Estimated 60-70 percent of consultees respond within statutory period
notes: PINS ministerial measures track valid submission rates as a proxy for consultee compliance

source_dataset: PPG Viability guidance and LPA monitoring
source_row_ref: PPG viability policy and pilot LPA viability assessment records
metric_name: Rate of viability re-assessment on allocated sites at application stage
baseline_value: Estimated 30-40 percent of allocated site applications subject to viability re-assessment
notes: Practitioner evidence and PPG monitoring indicate widespread reopening of plan-stage viability

source_row_ref: PINS NSIP pre-application and examination stage durations
metric_name: Pre-application consultation duration for medium NSIP schemes
baseline_value: Median pre-application period 18-24 months for energy and transport NSIP
notes: NSIP pre-application requirements under PA 2008 s42-s47 disproportionate for sub-1GW energy schemes

source_dataset: GOV.UK EIA statistics and PINS screening records
source_row_ref: EIA screening opinions and scoping requests
metric_name: EIA over-screening rate for smaller commercial and residential schemes
baseline_value: Estimated 20-30 percent of screened schemes are over-screened relative to actual impact
notes: EIA thresholds in Schedule 2 TCPA 1990 SI 2017/571 not updated for current development scale
